answer_text:
Push notifications not received on mobile app after release

Hypothesis 1: Mobile notifications may not be enabled in either the Example Product or the device operating system — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]
Hypothesis 2: There may be an issue related to the recent app release affecting notification functionality — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-2], [KB-3]

1. Confirm that mobile notifications are enabled in the Example Product settings. You can check this in the app under Settings > Notifications. [KB-1]
2. Check the device's operating system settings to ensure that notifications for the Example Product app are enabled. This can usually be found in Settings > Apps > Example Product > Notifications.
3. If notifications are enabled, sign out of the Example Product app and then sign back in to refresh the notification permissions. [KB-1]
4. If the issue persists after confirming the settings and signing in again, please check if there are any updates available for the app and install them if necessary.
5. To help us resolve this as quickly as possible, could you let us know if notifications are enabled in both the app and the device settings?

Subject: Push Notifications Issue on Mobile App

Hi [redacted_name],

It seems that the push notifications have stopped working on your mobile app, which could be due to mobile notifications not being enabled in either the Example Product or your device's operating system settings. To resolve this, please check that notifications are enabled in both places.

If they are enabled, try signing out of the app and signing back in to refresh the notification permissions. If the issue continues, please let us know if notifications are enabled in both the app and your device settings.

Kind regards,
Support Team

Issue Classification:
Push notifications not received on mobile app after release

Diagnosis:
Hypothesis 1: Mobile notifications may not be enabled in either the Example Product or the device operating system — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]
Hypothesis 2: There may be an issue related to the recent app release affecting notification functionality — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-2], [KB-3]

Root Cause:
The most likely cause for the lack of push notifications is that mobile notifications are not enabled in either the Example Product or the device's operating system settings. This is supported by the information in [KB-1]. There is also a possibility that the recent app release introduced issues affecting notifications, as indicated in [KB-2] and [KB-3].

Resolution Steps:
1. Confirm that mobile notifications are enabled in the Example Product settings. You can check this in the app under Settings > Notifications. [KB-1]
2. Check the device's operating system settings to ensure that notifications for the Example Product app are enabled. This can usually be found in Settings > Apps > Example Product > Notifications.
3. If notifications are enabled, sign out of the Example Product app and then sign back in to refresh the notification permissions. [KB-1]
4. If the issue persists after confirming the settings and signing in again, please check if there are any updates available for the app and install them if necessary.
5. To help us resolve this as quickly as possible, could you let us know if notifications are enabled in both the app and the device settings?

Sources:
demo_knowledge_base.csv, demo_release_notes.csv

Confidence:
MEDIUM

answer_text:
Mobile push notifications not received by agent

Hypothesis 1: Mobile notifications may not be properly enabled in the Example Product or device settings — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]
Hypothesis 2: The agent may not be assigned to any active conversations or the team inbox — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-1]

1. Confirm that mobile notifications are enabled in the Example Product settings. Check under the notifications section of the app. [KB-1]
2. Verify that mobile notifications are enabled in the device's operating system settings. This can usually be found in the app settings on the device. [KB-1]
3. If changes are made to the notification settings, ask Jordan to sign out of the app and then sign back in. [KB-1]
4. Check if Jordan is currently assigned to any active conversations or the team inbox. If not, this could be the reason for the lack of notifications. [KB-1]

Subject: Assistance with Mobile Push Notifications

Hi [redacted_name],

It seems that the reason you are not receiving mobile push notifications could be related to the notification settings in the Example Product or your device. To resolve this, please check that mobile notifications are enabled in both the app settings and your device's operating system. 

If you make any changes, please sign out of the app and sign back in. Additionally, ensure that you are assigned to any active conversations or the team inbox, as notifications are only sent for those.

Kind regards,
Support Team

Issue Classification:
Mobile push notifications not received by agent

Diagnosis:
Hypothesis 1: Mobile notifications may not be properly enabled in the Example Product or device settings — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]
Hypothesis 2: The agent may not be assigned to any active conversations or the team inbox — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-1]

Root Cause:
The most likely cause of Jordan not receiving mobile push notifications is that mobile notifications may not be properly enabled in either the Example Product or the device settings. This is supported by the information in [KB-1]. There is also a possibility that Jordan is not assigned to any active conversations or the team inbox, which could affect notification delivery.

Resolution Steps:
1. Confirm that mobile notifications are enabled in the Example Product settings. Check under the notifications section of the app. [KB-1]
2. Verify that mobile notifications are enabled in the device's operating system settings. This can usually be found in the app settings on the device. [KB-1]
3. If changes are made to the notification settings, ask Jordan to sign out of the app and then sign back in. [KB-1]
4. Check if Jordan is currently assigned to any active conversations or the team inbox. If not, this could be the reason for the lack of notifications. [KB-1]

HIGH

If you make any changes, please sign out of the app and sign back in. Additionally, ensure that you are assigned to any active conversations or the team inbox, as notifications are only sent for those.

2. Verify that mobile notifications are enabled in the device's operating system settings. This may vary depending on the device being used. [KB-1]
3. Ask Jordan to sign out of the Example Product app and then sign back in to refresh the notification settings. [KB-1]

Hi [redacted_name],

It seems that you are not receiving notifications for team inbox conversations after changing your availability. This could be due to mobile notifications not being enabled in either the Example Product or your device's operating system. 

Could you please check that mobile notifications are enabled in both places? Additionally, signing out of the app and signing back in may help refresh your notification settings.

Kind regards,
Support Team

Stale mobile badge count after conversation reassignment

Hypothesis 1: Delayed mobile badge counts — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]
Hypothesis 2: Mobile queued reply badge may persist — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-2]

1. Wait for up to five minutes to see if the mobile badge count updates automatically, as some agents may experience delays in badge counts after reassignment from a team inbox [KB-1].
2. If the badge count does not update after five minutes, try refreshing the app or logging out and back in to see if that resolves the issue [KB-1].
3. If the issue persists, check if there are any queued replies that may be causing the badge to remain pending [KB-2].

Subject: Stale Mobile Badge Count After Reassignment

Hi,

It seems that the stale mobile badge count you are experiencing is likely due to a known delay in mobile badge updates, which can last up to five minutes after a conversation is reassigned from a team inbox. To resolve this, please wait for up to five minutes to see if the badge count updates automatically. If it does not, try refreshing the app or logging out and back in.

If you continue to experience issues, please let me know.

Kind regards,
Support Team

Issue Classification:
Stale mobile badge count after conversation reassignment

Hypothesis 2: Mobile queued reply badge may persist — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-2]

Root Cause:
The most likely cause of the stale mobile badge count is a delay in the mobile badge updates, which can last up to five minutes after a conversation is reassigned from a team inbox. This is supported by [KB-1]. An alternative cause could be that a queued reply badge persists even after the reply has synced, as mentioned in [KB-2].

Resolution Steps:
1. Wait for up to five minutes to see if the mobile badge count updates automatically, as some agents may experience delays in badge counts after reassignment from a team inbox [KB-1].
2. If the badge count does not update after five minutes, try refreshing the app or logging out and back in to see if that resolves the issue [KB-1].
3. If the issue persists, check if there are any queued replies that may be causing the badge to remain pending [KB-2].

HIGH

answer_text:
Session timeout causing role change failure

Hypothesis 1: The user was signed out due to session timeout after eight hours of inactivity, which can prevent completing a role change. — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]
Hypothesis 2: The role change may have failed due to browser settings blocking third-party cookies, which can affect functionality. — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-1]

1. Please refresh your browser and sign in again to ensure you have an active session before attempting the role change. This is recommended after a session timeout, as stated in [KB-1].
2. If the role change still fails after signing in, please check if your browser is blocking third-party cookies for Example Product. Adjust your browser settings to allow these cookies if necessary, as this can impact functionality according to [KB-1].
3. If you continue to experience issues, please confirm your current role and any specific error messages you encounter when attempting the role change.

Subject: Assistance with Role Change Issue

Hi [redacted_name],

It seems that you may have been signed out due to session timeout after leaving the admin page open for an extended period. This can prevent you from completing a role change. 

To resolve this, please refresh your browser and sign in again before trying the role change. If you still encounter issues, check your browser settings to ensure that third-party cookies for Example Product are not being blocked, as this can affect functionality.

If you continue to have trouble, please let me know your current role and any specific error messages you see when attempting the change.

Kind regards,
Support Team

Issue Classification:
Session timeout causing role change failure

Diagnosis:
Hypothesis 1: The user was signed out due to session timeout after eight hours of inactivity, which can prevent completing a role change. — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]
Hypothesis 2: The role change may have failed due to browser settings blocking third-party cookies, which can affect functionality. — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-1]

Root Cause:
The most likely cause of the issue is that Maya was signed out due to session timeout after leaving the admin page open for an extended period. This is supported by the information in [KB-1]. An alternative cause could be browser settings that block third-party cookies, which could also interfere with the role change process.

Resolution Steps:
1. Please refresh your browser and sign in again to ensure you have an active session before attempting the role change. This is recommended after a session timeout, as stated in [KB-1].
2. If the role change still fails after signing in, please check if your browser is blocking third-party cookies for Example Product. Adjust your browser settings to allow these cookies if necessary, as this can impact functionality according to [KB-1].
3. If you continue to experience issues, please confirm your current role and any specific error messages you encounter when attempting the role change.

answer_text:
Trial workspace data restoration request after retention period

Hypothesis 1: The trial workspace data cannot be restored because it has been more than 30 days since the trial expired — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]

1. Confirm the exact date the trial workspace [redacted_account_id] to ensure it has indeed been over 30 days.
2. If the trial expired within the last 30 days, consider upgrading the workspace or exporting any remaining data during the retention period.
3. Unfortunately, if the trial expired more than 30 days ago, data restoration is not possible as per policy ([KB-1]).

Subject: Trial Workspace Data Restoration Request

Hi,

I see that you're requesting to restore data from a trial workspace that expired more than 30 days ago. Unfortunately, once the trial period ends, the data is retained for only 30 days, and after that, it cannot be restored by support ().

To help us confirm this, could you let us know the exact date the trial workspace [redacted_account_id]? If it was within the last 30 days, we can explore options for upgrading the workspace or exporting any remaining data.

Kind regards,
Support Team

Issue Classification:
Trial workspace data restoration request after retention period

Diagnosis:
Hypothesis 1: The trial workspace data cannot be restored because it has been more than 30 days since the trial expired — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]

Root Cause:
The most likely cause of the issue is that the trial workspace data cannot be restored because it has been more than 30 days since the trial expired. According to the knowledge base, trial workspaces are retained for only 30 days after expiration, and once this period ends, deleted data cannot be restored by support ([KB-1]).

Resolution Steps:
1. Confirm the exact date the trial workspace [redacted_account_id] to ensure it has indeed been over 30 days.
2. If the trial expired within the last 30 days, consider upgrading the workspace or exporting any remaining data during the retention period.
3. Unfortunately, if the trial expired more than 30 days ago, data restoration is not possible as per policy ([KB-1]).

Sources:
demo_policies.csv

Confidence:
HIGH

To help us confirm this, could you let us know the exact date the trial workspace [redacted_account_id]? If it was within the last 30 days, we can explore options for upgrading the workspace or exporting any remaining data.

Support Team

Export issue — missing export ready email

Hypothesis 1: The export may have completed successfully, but the admin did not receive the export ready email due to strict mail gateway settings. — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-3]
Hypothesis 2: The user may not have the necessary permissions to request an export, which could lead to confusion about the export status. — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-2]

1. Confirm whether the export was requested and completed successfully. If possible, check the Exports section in the admin panel to see if the export is listed.
2. If the export is listed, check the email settings and confirm if the admin's email domain is on a strict mail gateway that might block the export ready email. This is supported by [KB-3].
3. If the export is not listed or if there are issues with permissions, verify that the user has the necessary role (admin or compliance manager) to request exports as per [KB-2].
4. If the user is not an admin or compliance manager, they will need to contact an admin to request the export or obtain temporary export permission.

Subject: Assistance with Your Export Issue

Hi,

It seems that your export may have completed successfully, but you might not have received the export ready email due to strict mail gateway settings. To help us resolve this as quickly as possible, could you let us know if you can see the export listed in the Exports section of your admin panel?

If you don't see it, we may need to check your permissions to ensure you have the necessary role to request exports.

Kind regards,
Support Team

Hypothesis 2: The user may not have the necessary permissions to request an export, which could lead to confusion about the export status. — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-2]

Root Cause:
The most likely cause of the issue is that the export completed successfully, but the admin did not receive the export ready email because of strict mail gateway settings. This is supported by [KB-3]. There is also a possibility that the user lacks the necessary permissions to request an export, as indicated in [KB-2].

Resolution Steps:
1. Confirm whether the export was requested and completed successfully. If possible, check the Exports section in the admin panel to see if the export is listed.
2. If the export is listed, check the email settings and confirm if the admin's email domain is on a strict mail gateway that might block the export ready email. This is supported by [KB-3].
3. If the export is not listed or if there are issues with permissions, verify that the user has the necessary role (admin or compliance manager) to request exports as per [KB-2].
4. If the user is not an admin or compliance manager, they will need to contact an admin to request the export or obtain temporary export permission.

3. Ask the agent to sign out of the app and then sign back in after making any changes to the notification permissions, as this may help refresh the settings.
4. Verify that the agent is assigned to conversations or team inboxes to ensure they are eligible to receive notifications.

To help us resolve this as quickly as possible, could you let us know if mobile notifications are currently enabled in both places?

Once we have that information, we can guide you through the next steps.

answer_text:
Mobile login 403 error on app — desktop login successful

Hypothesis 1: The mobile app session token may need to be refreshed after an update — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]
Hypothesis 2: There could be an issue with the user’s role or permissions affecting mobile access — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-1]

1. Ask the user to log out of the mobile app and then log back in to refresh the session token. This step is based on the guidance from [KB-1].
2. If the login still fails, confirm the user's employee role, collect the app version, and the device operating system. This information is necessary for further diagnosis as per [KB-1].
3. If the issue persists after gathering the above information, escalate the ticket to support with the timestamp of the latest failed mobile login attempt.

Subject: Assistance with Mobile Login Issue

Hi,

It seems that you are experiencing a 403 error when trying to log in to the mobile app, while the desktop version works fine. This issue often occurs when the mobile app session token needs to be refreshed after an update. Please try logging out of the app and then logging back in to see if that resolves the issue.

If you still encounter problems, could you please confirm your employee role, as well as provide the app version and your device's operating system? This information will help us assist you further.

Kind regards,  
Support Team

Issue Classification:
Mobile login 403 error on app — desktop login successful

Diagnosis:
Hypothesis 1: The mobile app session token may need to be refreshed after an update — Likelihood: HIGH — Evidence: [KB-1]
Hypothesis 2: There could be an issue with the user’s role or permissions affecting mobile access — Likelihood: MEDIUM — Evidence: [KB-1]

Root Cause:
The most likely cause of the 403 error on mobile login is that the mobile app session token needs to be refreshed after an update, as indicated in [KB-1]. If this does not resolve the issue, it may be related to the user's role or permissions.

Resolution Steps:
1. Ask the user to log out of the mobile app and then log back in to refresh the session token. This step is based on the guidance from [KB-1].
2. If the login still fails, confirm the user's employee role, collect the app version, and the device operating system. This information is necessary for further diagnosis as per [KB-1].
3. If the issue persists after gathering the above information, escalate the ticket to support with the timestamp of the latest failed mobile login attempt.

Sources:
demo_knowledge_base.csv

Confidence:
MEDIUM
